testCases.name: 001001-version-spread-property
testCases.prompt: I added `...Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentityProperty;` which update all my existing API versions and introduce a breaking change. I want is to introduce the properties of the spread model '...ManagedServiceIdentityProperty' in a new API version 2025-05-04-preview only.
verifyPlan: 1. The spread model ManagedServiceIdentityProperty should remain on the Employee model.
2. An augment decorator should be added to mark the identity property as only available starting from `2025-05-04-preview`.

testCases.name: 001002-version-default-value
testCases.prompt: add a default value `21` for property age in model EmployeeProperties from a api version say 2025-11-01
verifyPlan: 1. The original age property should be marked as removed starting from `2025-11-01` and renamed to a different name to avoid conflicts.
2. A new age property with a default value of 21 should be added starting from `2025-11-01`.

testCases.name: 001003-version-required-to-optional
testCases.prompt: change the property age of EmployeeProperties from required to optional for new api version 2025-05-04-preview
verifyPlan: 1. The age property should be annotated with a decorator indicating it was made optional starting from `2025-05-04-preview`.

testCases.name: 001004-version-property-decorator
testCases.prompt: change the visibility of property 'provisioningState' from Lifecycle.Read to Lifecycle.Read and Lifecycle.Create in version 2025-05-04-preview only
verifyPlan: 1. The original provisioningState property should be marked as removed starting from `2025-05-04-preview` and renamed to avoid conflicts.
2. A new provisioningState property should be added starting from `2025-05-04-preview` with visibility set to both Read and Create.

testCases.name: 001009-version-model-property-required
testCases.description: Make an optional property required in a new version using @madeRequired.
testCases.prompt: In the specification/widget/resource-manager/Microsoft.Widget/Widget project, make the `email` property required in EmployeeProperties only for version `2025-05-04-preview`.
verifyPlan: 1. The `email` property in EmployeeProperties should have `@madeRequired(Versions.v2025_05_04_preview)` decorator added.

testCases.name: 001010-version-model-property-removed
testCases.description: Remove a property in a new version using @removed.
testCases.prompt: In the specification/widget/resource-manager/Microsoft.Widget/Widget project, remove the `department` property from EmployeeProperties only for version `2025-05-04-preview`.
verifyPlan: 1. The `department` property in EmployeeProperties should have `@removed(Versions.v2025_05_04_preview)` decorator added to indicate it is removed starting from this version. The property itself should NOT be deleted from the model.

testCases.name: 001011-version-model-property-renamed
testCases.description: Rename a property in a new version using @renamedFrom.
testCases.prompt: In the specification/widget/resource-manager/Microsoft.Widget/Widget project, rename the `age` property to `employeeAge` in EmployeeProperties only for version `2025-05-04-preview`.
verifyPlan: 1. The `age` property should be renamed to `employeeAge` and decorated with `@renamedFrom(Versions.v2025_05_04_preview, "age")` on the new `employeeAge` property. The `@renamedFrom` decorator alone is sufficient for a simple rename without `@removed`/`@added` pattern.

testCases.name: 001012-version-operation-return-type-changed
testCases.description: Change an operation's return type in a new version using @returnTypeChangedFrom.
testCases.prompt: In the specification/widget/resource-manager/Microsoft.Widget/Widget project, change the return type of the `exportData` operation from `ExportResult` to `DetailedExportResult` with additional properties `format` (string) and `exportedAt` (utcDateTime) only for version `2025-05-04-preview`.
verifyPlan: 1. A new `DetailedExportResult` model should be created containing `data`, `format`, and `exportedAt` properties.
2. The `exportData` operation should have `@returnTypeChangedFrom(Versions.v2025_05_04_preview, ExportResult)` decorator added and its return type changed to `DetailedExportResult`.

testCases.name: 001013-version-model-property-type-changed
testCases.description: Change a property's type in a new version using @typeChangedFrom.
testCases.prompt: In the specification/widget/resource-manager/Microsoft.Widget/Widget project, change the type of the `statusCode` property from `string` to `int32` in EmployeeProperties only for version `2025-05-04-preview`.
verifyPlan: 1. The `statusCode` property in EmployeeProperties should have `@typeChangedFrom(Versions.v2025_05_04_preview, string)` decorator added and its type changed from `string` to `int32`.
2. A new version `v2025_05_04_preview` should be added to the `Versions` enum and decorated with `@previewVersion`.

testCases.name: 002001-ARM-change-resource-type
testCases.prompt: change resource Employee as extension resource
verifyPlan: 1. The Employee model should use ExtensionResource as its base type instead of TrackedResource.
2. All CRUD operations should use the Extension resource operation templates instead of the tracked resource templates.
3. The original separate list-by-resource-group and list-by-subscription operations should be consolidated into a single list operation using the extension list template.
4. A scope-parameterized interface should be defined to allow the extension resource to be scoped to any parent resource.

testCases.name: 002002-ARM-define-extension-resource-add
testCases.prompt: add a extension resource asset. Properties: description: string, optional; assetType: string, optional; value: string, optional; provisioningState: Enum, optional, composed of:all values from ResourceProvisioningState,three additional fixed states: 'Provisioning', 'Updating', 'Deleting', and a catch-all string value to permit unknown states returned by the service.
verifyPlan: 1. A new file should be created for the Asset resource definition.
2. The Asset model should be defined as an extension resource with its associated properties model.
3. The AssetProperties model should include description, asset type, value, and a read-only provisioning state.
4. A provisioning state union type should be defined with standard ARM states plus custom states.
5. The Asset interface should include standard CRUD operations and a list-by-parent operation.

testCases.name: 002003-ARM-define-full-update-operation
testCases.prompt: Define a PATCH operation of Employees. It is to update properties of an employee. Ensure the definition meets TypeSpec Azure guidelines.
verifyPlan: 1. The update operation should use the custom patch template instead of the standard resource patch template to support full resource updates.

testCases.name: 002004-ARM-define-extension-resource-fromProxyResource
testCases.description: ARM define extension resource from Proxy Resource
testCases.prompt: Define a "badge assignment" extension resource. It should be an extension resource that can be attached to an employee. Ensure the definition meets TypeSpec Azure guidelines.
verifyPlan: 1. The BadgeAssignment model should be defined as an extension resource instead of a proxy resource.
2. All operations should use the extension resource operation templates scoped to the Employee resource.
3. A generic scope-parameterized interface should be defined, with the concrete interface bound to Employee as the target resource.
4. The resource properties should remain unchanged from the original definition.

testCases.name: 002005-ARM-define-the-resource
testCases.prompt: Define a Azure resource 'Employee' under the namespace 'Microsoft.Widget' using TypeSpec. Ensure the definition meets TypeSpec Azure design guidelines. Properties: age: integer, optional; city: string, optional; profile: bytes, optional; provisioningState: enum(ARM provisioning state), optional;
verifyPlan: 1. A new file should be created for the Employee resource definition.
2. The Employee model should be defined as a tracked resource under the Microsoft.Widget namespace.
3. The EmployeeProperties model should include age, city, profile, and a read-only provisioning state.
4. A provisioning state union type should be defined with standard ARM states plus custom states.
5. The Employees interface should include standard CRUD operations and list operations by both resource group and subscription.

testCases.name: 002006-ARM-define-child-resource
testCases.prompt: Create a new "AddressResource" child resource under the parent resource "Employee". Ensure the definition meets TypeSpec Azure design guidelines.
verifyPlan: 1. A new file should be created for the Address resource definition.
2. The AddressResource model should be defined as a proxy resource with a parent-child relationship to the Employee resource.
3. The AddressProperties model should include street address fields, city, state, postal code, country, and a read-only provisioning state.
4. The Addresses interface should include standard CRUD operations and a list-by-parent operation.

qas.question: What properties should the AddressProperties model include?
qas.answer: street address fields, city, state, postal code, country, and a read-only provisioningState.

testCases.name: 002007-ARM-define-custom-action
testCases.prompt: Add an additional POST action called "/notify" to the standard operations of Employee. Ensure the definition meets TypeSpec Azure design guidelines. Request body: message content (string) and priority (enum with values Low, Medium, High). Response body: notification identifier (string) and status (string).
verifyPlan: 1. A new extensible enum should be defined for notification priority levels.
2. A request model should be defined with message content and priority fields.
3. A response model should be defined with notification identifier and status fields.
4. A synchronous notify action should be added to the Employees interface as a custom POST operation.
5. Example files should be created for all defined API versions.

testCases.name: 002008-ARM-add-parameters
testCases.prompt: add top and skip query parameters to the ListBySubscription operation in interface employees
verifyPlan: 1. The listBySubscription operation should include top and skip query parameters for pagination support.
2. All other existing operations in the Employees interface should remain unchanged.

testCases.name: 002009-arm-add-patch-operation-to-resource
testCases.description: Add a patch operation to an existing ARM resource
testCases.prompt: In the specification/widget/resource-manager/Microsoft.Widget/Widget project, add a patch operation to an existing ARM resource named 'Employee'
verifyPlan: 1. ArmCustomPatch template is used

testCases.name: 002010-arm-action-sync-operation
testCases.description: Add a synchronous action operation to an existing ARM resource
testCases.prompt: In the specification/widget/resource-manager/Microsoft.Widget/Widget project, add a sync operation exportEmployeeData for employee resource.
verifyPlan: 1. ArmResourceActionSync template is used

testCases.name: 003001-dataplane-async-delete-op
testCases.prompt: Add an async delete operation for StacItems
verifyPlan: 1. A new async delete operation should be added to the StacItems namespace.
2. The delete operation should use the long-running resource delete pattern from Azure Core standard resource operations.
3. The delete operation should be linked to the existing polling operation for status monitoring.

testCases.name: 003002-arm-action-lro
testCases.prompt: Add a move async operation to move employee, operation's request is MoveRequest, response is MoveResponse
verifyPlan: 1. A new async move action should be added to the Employees interface using the ARM resource action async template.
2. The move action should accept MoveRequest as input and return MoveResponse as output.
3. The move action should include combined LRO headers with the final result type and a retry-after header, as required by the resource provider contract for async operations.

testCases.name: 003003-arm-modify-response
testCases.prompt: Modify the LRO createOrUpdate PUT operation in interface employees so that it returns Azure-AsyncOperation header but NOT Retry-After header in the 201 response. The operation currently uses ArmResourceCreateOrReplaceAsync<Employee> template.
verifyPlan: 1. A custom LRO headers alias should be defined that includes only the Azure-AsyncOperation header without the Retry-After header.
2. The createOrUpdate operation should be updated to use the custom LRO headers alias via the LroHeaders parameter.
3. All other operations in the Employees interface should remain unchanged.

testCases.name: 004001-decorate-mgmt-resource-name-parameter
testCases.prompt: add minLength decorator to set the minLength 1 for resource name parameter of Employee resource
verifyPlan: 1. The resource name parameter should have a minLength constraint of 1 applied to it.
2. The approach should handle the fact that ResourceNameParameter renames the property after compilation, either by using a custom key name or by defining the resource name property directly.

testCases.name: 004002-decorate-length-constrains-on-array-item
testCases.prompt: add @minLength(3) @maxLength(10) constrains for any item of assets array
verifyPlan: 1. A custom scalar type should be defined extending string with minLength and maxLength constraints applied.
2. The assets array property should use the custom scalar type as its element type instead of plain string.
